element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player drives selected sports car on chosen track
Avoidance required; collision penalty unknown but implied as negative
Evading police; three tickets result in arrest (two in Saturn version)
Altered car dynamics for faster, less realistic driving
Bonus vehicle with unique performance characteristics

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Point-to-point courses create linear tension buildup from start to finish; police mechanic introduces dynamic pressure during races; three-section structure (3DO version) provides intermediate relief points

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Lost Vegas track unlocked by winning all standard tournaments

difficulty_curve.early_game: Initial track selection allows player to choose difficulty implicitly; basic racing mechanics introduced without forced challenge
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Police pursuits and traffic avoidance become consistent mechanics; tournament progression implies increasing AI opponent competence
difficulty_curve.late_game: Tournament completion unlocks hidden Lost Vegas track and rally mode; Warrior PTO E/2 jet car provides post-game content
notable_spikes: First police encounter during a race
Alpine track introduces mountain terrain complexity
Lost Vegas unlock condition (complete all tournaments)

notable_level_patterns.description: Original 3DO tracks (City, Coastal, Alpine) divided into three stages each; linear progression from start to finish with intermediate checkpoints
Later ports include circuit-based tracks (Rusty Springs, Autumn Valley, Vertigo) for looping gameplay without point-to-point structure
Six distinctive environments provide visual/gameplay contrast; each track has unique terrain and hazard profiles
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All six tracks (City, Coastal, Alpine, Rusty Springs, Autumn Valley, Vertigo)

notable_level_patterns.description: Lost Vegas track unlocked only after winning all tournaments; serves as post-game challenge or exploration reward
